title: Rinorea engleriana
taxonomy: Rinorea engleriana is classified within the family Violaceae. This family belongs to the order Malpighiales. The order Malpighiales is part of the clade eurosids I. This clade is a subgroup of the larger clade rosids. Further, rosids are a major clade of flowering plants within the division Magnoliophyta.

However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal potential and its effects on human health.

intro: Rinorea engleriana is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Africa, belonging to the family Violaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and studies have shown that it possesses various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may help to alleviate symptoms of infections, wounds, and other inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Rinorea engleriana include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Rinorea engleriana is often used to prepare herbal infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and gastrointestinal issues.
